# Supravisor
> Supravisor is the trust layer for AI agents, powered by the open Uniplex protocol.

Supravisor gives every AI agent a cryptographic identity (Passport), defines enforcement rules for what it can do (Gates + Guardrails), and produces a signed, immutable record of every decision (Attestations). It's the infrastructure layer that makes AI agents safe to deploy at scale — locally or in production, with zero network latency for authorization decisions.

## Docs

- https://docs.uniplex.ai/for-humans — Plain-English guide, no code required
- https://docs.uniplex.ai/concepts/issuers — Passport-signing authorities
- https://docs.uniplex.ai/concepts/passports — Agent credentials (Ed25519 signed)
- https://docs.uniplex.ai/concepts/gates — Local enforcement checkpoints
- https://docs.uniplex.ai/concepts/guardrails — 17 constraint evaluators
- https://docs.uniplex.ai/concepts/trust-profiles — L1/L2/L3 security levels
- https://docs.uniplex.ai/concepts/attestations — Cryptographic proof records
- https://docs.uniplex.ai/guides/protect-claude — Claude and Claude Code integration
- https://docs.uniplex.ai/guides/protect-openai — OpenAI Agents SDK integration
- https://docs.uniplex.ai/guides/mcp-server — 21 MCP tools for AI client management
- https://docs.uniplex.ai/guides/agent-autonomy — Autonomous agent credential management
- https://docs.uniplex.ai/guides/custom-constraints — Building custom guardrails
- https://docs.uniplex.ai/guides/audit-trail — Reading and sharing audit records
- https://docs.uniplex.ai/sdk/python — Python SDK reference
- https://docs.uniplex.ai/sdk/typescript — TypeScript/Node.js SDK reference
- https://docs.uniplex.ai/templates/overview — 30 ready-to-use agent profiles
- https://docs.uniplex.ai/api-reference/authentication — API keys and scopes
- https://docs.uniplex.ai/api-reference/passports — Passports REST API
- https://docs.uniplex.ai/api-reference/gates — Gates REST API
- https://docs.uniplex.ai/api-reference/guardrails — Guardrails REST API
- https://docs.uniplex.ai/api-reference/audit-trail — Audit Trail REST API

## Key Facts

- **Passports** are Ed25519-signed JSON credentials carrying agent_id, permissions, issuer_id, public_key, and expiry
- **Passport ID format**: `uni_xxxxxxxx` (64 hex chars after prefix)
- **Gates** verify passports locally — no network call, sub-millisecond — then evaluate guardrail constraints
- **17 constraint evaluators** cover: spending caps (per-action, per-day, cumulative), rate limits (per-minute, per-hour), domain allowlist/blocklist, action allowlist/blocklist, read_only mode, PII export protection, required fields, model enforcement, and SLA tracking
- **Trust Profiles**: L1 (self-issued, dev), L2 (managed issuer, nonce replay protection, production), L3 (proof-of-possession, live revocation, high-stakes)
- **Attestations** are Ed25519-signed by the gate's private key — immutable, independently verifiable, tamper-proof
- **Issuer format**: `issuer:yourdomain.ai` — acts like a certificate authority for agents
- **Private keys** are generated client-side in the browser and never stored by Supravisor
- **Revocation** is instant — gates reject revoked passports immediately (L2: cached; L3: live check)
- **MCP server** provides 21 tools via Streamable HTTP transport with OAuth 2.0 PKCE authentication
- **API base URL**: `https://uniplex.ai/api`
- **API key format**: `uni_xxxx` — used as Bearer token in Authorization header
- **30 constraint templates** across 5 categories: Personal Productivity, Business Operations, Developer Tools, Finance & Commerce, Research & Content
- **Permission namespaces**: `mcp:*`, `web:*`, `email:*`, `files:*`, `payments:*`, `api:*`, `tools:*`, `*`
- The **UNIPLEX_PASSPORT_KEY** environment variable stores the agent's private key
- Agents can operate fully autonomously after a one-time human bootstrap

## SDK

```bash
# Python
pip install uniplex

# TypeScript / Node.js
npm install uniplex
```

**Python quick start:**
```python
from uniplex import Agent, Gate, TrustProfile
agent = Agent.from_env()          # reads UNIPLEX_PASSPORT_KEY
gate = Gate(profile=TrustProfile.L2, trusted_issuers=["issuer:mycompany.ai"])
decision = gate.authorize(agent.generate_token(), "web:search")
print(decision.allowed, decision.reason, decision.attestation_id)
```

**TypeScript quick start:**
```typescript
import { Agent, Gate, TrustProfile } from 'uniplex';
const agent = await Agent.fromEnv();
const gate = new Gate({ profile: TrustProfile.L2, trustedIssuers: ['issuer:mycompany.ai'] });
const decision = await gate.authorize(agent.generateToken(), 'web:search');
console.log(decision.allowed, decision.reason, decision.attestationId);
```

**Self-issued (no account needed):**
```python
from uniplex import Agent
agent = Agent.create("my-agent", permissions=["mcp:*", "web:search"])
decision = agent.authorize("mcp:search")
```

## MCP Server

```bash
npx uniplex-mcp-manage
```

21 tools: create_issuer, list_issuers, get_issuer, update_issuer, revoke_issuer, issue_passport, list_passports, get_passport, revoke_passport, create_gate, list_gates, get_gate, update_gate, delete_gate, check_authorization, verify_passport, list_attestations, get_attestation, export_attestations, list_templates, apply_template

OAuth 2.0 PKCE authentication. Add to Claude Desktop or Claude Code via MCP config.

## API

**Base URL:** `https://uniplex.ai/api`

**Authentication:** `Authorization: Bearer uni_xxxx`

**Key endpoints:**

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| POST | /api/authorize | Check if a passport is authorized for an action |
| POST | /api/passports | Issue a new passport |
| GET | /api/passports | List passports |
| DELETE | /api/passports/:id | Revoke a passport |
| POST | /api/issuers | Create an issuer |
| GET | /api/issuers | List issuers |
| POST | /api/gates | Create a gate |
| GET | /api/gates | List gates |
| PUT | /api/gates/:id | Update gate constraints |
| GET | /api/audit-trail | List attestations with filters |
| GET | /api/audit-trail/:id | Get single attestation |
| POST | /api/audit-trail/:id/share | Create public share link |
| GET | /api/audit-trail/export | Export as JSON or CSV |

**Rate limits:** 100/min (authorization), 30/min (passport creation), 10/min (gate creation)

**Error format:** `{"error": "message", "code": "ERROR_CODE"}`

**Common error codes:** UNAUTHORIZED, FORBIDDEN, PASSPORT_EXPIRED, PASSPORT_REVOKED, ISSUER_NOT_TRUSTED, PERMISSION_DENIED, CONSTRAINT_VIOLATION, SIGNATURE_INVALID
